name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America is a national payday and installment lender offering same-day personal loans up to $1,000 and title loans up to $25,000 in-store and online.
description_long:
Advance America is one of the largest and most established short-term consumer lenders in the United States, operating thousands of physical locations nationwide alongside a full online lending platform. The Jacksonville, FL location on N. Edgewood Ave. serves the Commonwealth Village area and surrounding west-of-downtown neighborhoods. The company describes itself as nationally recognized and fully accredited, with over 200,000 Google reviews and a 4.9-out-of-5 aggregate rating across 125,409 verified customer reviews.

Advance America offers three core loan products at this location. Payday Loans range from $100 to $500 and are structured as two-to-four-week short-term loans intended to bridge gaps between paychecks. Installment Loans range from $100 to $1,000 and allow repayment across multiple payments over a longer term, making them more manageable than single-payment payday products. Title Loans, offered in partnership with LoanCenter, go from $2,000 up to $25,000 and use the borrower's paid-off vehicle as collateral. All three products are available online or in-store, with phone pre-qualification also offered. The store additionally provides Western Union money transfer services.

Several features differentiate this location from generic payday lenders. Spanish-speaking staff are on-site, serving Jacksonville's bilingual population. The combination of three loan types under one roof — payday, installment, and title — gives borrowers more flexibility than single-product storefronts. The online application pathway means customers do not need to visit in person for every loan type. The referral program provides rewards for existing customers who bring in new borrowers. Store hours extend to Saturday, though the location is closed on Sundays.

Honestly assessed, Advance America occupies the high-cost, short-term lending space. Payday loans at $500 maximum are a limited solution for meaningful emergencies, and their 2-4 week repayment structure can create pressure for borrowers without reliable cash flow. Title loans carry the significant risk of vehicle repossession if payments are missed, and they require a fully paid-off car registered in the borrower's name. Advance America does not publish APR ranges on this store page, which is a notable transparency gap — prospective borrowers should request full cost disclosures before signing. For consumers who have exhausted lower-cost options, Advance America's combination of in-store service, same-day funding, and multiple loan structures makes it one of the more flexible operators in the emergency cash segment.
pros: Same-day personal loans available up to $1,000 in-store
Three loan types at one location: payday, installment, and title loans
Online and in-store application options with phone pre-qualification
Title loans up to $25,000 via LoanCenter partnership
4.9/5 stars across 125,409 verified reviews and 200,000+ Google reviews
Western Union money transfer available in-store
cons: Payday loans capped at $500 — insufficient for larger emergencies
Payday loans have 2-4 week repayment windows that can create repeat borrowing cycles
Title loans require a fully paid-off vehicle registered in your name — risk of repossession
No APR or fee disclosures on the store page; borrowers must ask in-store
Closed Sundays; limited to 4:00 pm on Saturdays
best_for: Borrowers needing $100–$500 before their next paycheck with no time for a bank loan
Vehicle owners with a paid-off car seeking larger emergency funds ($2K–$25K)
Spanish-speaking consumers who need in-person bilingual loan assistance
Consumers who prefer in-store service with same-day cash over online-only lenders
diagnosis: Advance America is best for consumers who need fast cash — up to $1,000 same-day — and have exhausted lower-cost options like credit unions or employer advances. Vehicle owners with a paid-off car have access to significantly larger loan amounts through the title loan product. The main caveat is cost: short-term lenders in this category typically carry triple-digit APRs, and Advance America does not disclose rates on its store pages, so borrowers must ask directly before committing.

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
